function.arguments:
{"command": "create", "path": "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/verification/scenarios/scenario_sram_memory.yaml", "file_text": "scenario_name: scenario_sram_memory
description: >
  Verifies the 128 KB SRAM subsystem (4 × CF_SRAM_1024x32 banks) of
  mspm33c321a_derivative. Covers base-address read/write, full address
  walking over the 128 KB range, sequential and random data-integrity
  patterns, and concurrent CPU + DMA access without corruption.
checklist_ids:
  - MEM-01
  - MEM-02
  - MEM-03
  - MEM-04
test_names:
  - test_mem_base_rw
  - test_mem_addr_walk
  - test_mem_data_integrity
  - test_mem_cpu_dma_concurrent
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices: []

sram_params:
  base_addr: 0x20000000
  size_bytes: 0x00020000   # 128 KB
  bank_count: 4
  bank_size_bytes: 0x00008000   # 32 KB each

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: release_reset
    description: Release reset and allow bus fabric to initialise
  - step: 2
    action: ahb_write_word
    addr: 0x20000000
    data: 0xDEADBEEF
    description: Write known word at SRAM base address
  - step: 3
    action: ahb_read_word
    addr: 0x20000000
    expected: 0xDEADBEEF
    description: Read back base address and verify data
  - step: 4
    action: ahb_address_walk
    start_addr: 0x20000000
    end_addr: 0x2001FFFF
    pattern: ADDR_AS_DATA
    description: Write address-as-data to every word; read back and compare
  - step: 5
    action: ahb_burst_write
    start_addr: 0x20000000
    length_words: 32768
    pattern: RANDOM_SEED_0xCAFE
    description: Sequential burst write with pseudo-random data (LFSR seed 0xCAFE)
  - step: 6
    action: ahb_burst_read_check
    start_addr: 0x20000000
    length_words: 32768
    pattern: RANDOM_SEED_0xCAFE
    description: Burst read and compare against expected LFSR sequence
  - step: 7
    action: concurrent_dma_cpu_access
    dma_channel: 0
    dma_src: 0x20000000
    dma_dst: 0x20010000
    dma_len_bytes: 0x8000
    cpu_rw_addr: 0x20018000
    description: CPU reads/writes upper SRAM bank while DMA copies lower bank

checks:
  - id: CHK-MEM-01
    checklist_id: MEM-01
    description: Read-back of 0xDEADBEEF at base address 0x20000000 matches write
    expected: data == 0xDEADBEEF
  - id: CHK-MEM-02
    checklist_id: MEM-02
    description: All 32768 word addresses in 128 KB read back their written value
    expected: Zero mismatches in full address walk
  - id: CHK-MEM-03
    checklist_id: MEM-03
    description: All 32768 LFSR-pattern words compare correctly after burst write
    expected: Zero byte mismatches
  - id: CHK-MEM-04
    checklist_id: MEM-04
    description: DMA copy completes correctly and CPU data is uncorrupted
    expected: DMA destination matches source; CPU reads are consistent

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-MEM checks PASS
  - No AHB HRESP=ERROR during SRAM access in valid range
  - DMA transfer-complete flag set within expected cycle count
"}
function.name: str_replace_editor

args.file_text:
scenario_name: scenario_sram_memory
description: >
  Verifies the 128 KB SRAM subsystem (4 × CF_SRAM_1024x32 banks) of
  mspm33c321a_derivative. Covers base-address read/write, full address
  walking over the 128 KB range, sequential and random data-integrity
  patterns, and concurrent CPU + DMA access without corruption.
checklist_ids:
  - MEM-01
  - MEM-02
  - MEM-03
  - MEM-04
test_names:
  - test_mem_base_rw
  - test_mem_addr_walk
  - test_mem_data_integrity
  - test_mem_cpu_dma_concurrent
priority: P0
dut:
  top_module: mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss
  filelist: rtl/compute_ss/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f

clock:
  name: clk
  period_ns: 12.5

reset:
  name: reset_n
  active_low: true
  assert_cycles: 20
  deassert_cycles: 5

external_devices: []

sram_params:
  base_addr: 0x20000000
  size_bytes: 0x00020000   # 128 KB
  bank_count: 4
  bank_size_bytes: 0x00008000   # 32 KB each

stimulus:
  - step: 1
    action: release_reset
    description: Release reset and allow bus fabric to initialise
  - step: 2
    action: ahb_write_word
    addr: 0x20000000
    data: 0xDEADBEEF
    description: Write known word at SRAM base address
  - step: 3
    action: ahb_read_word
    addr: 0x20000000
    expected: 0xDEADBEEF
    description: Read back base address and verify data
  - step: 4
    action: ahb_address_walk
    start_addr: 0x20000000
    end_addr: 0x2001FFFF
    pattern: ADDR_AS_DATA
    description: Write address-as-data to every word; read back and compare
  - step: 5
    action: ahb_burst_write
    start_addr: 0x20000000
    length_words: 32768
    pattern: RANDOM_SEED_0xCAFE
    description: Sequential burst write with pseudo-random data (LFSR seed 0xCAFE)
  - step: 6
    action: ahb_burst_read_check
    start_addr: 0x20000000
    length_words: 32768
    pattern: RANDOM_SEED_0xCAFE
    description: Burst read and compare against expected LFSR sequence
  - step: 7
    action: concurrent_dma_cpu_access
    dma_channel: 0
    dma_src: 0x20000000
    dma_dst: 0x20010000
    dma_len_bytes: 0x8000
    cpu_rw_addr: 0x20018000
    description: CPU reads/writes upper SRAM bank while DMA copies lower bank

checks:
  - id: CHK-MEM-01
    checklist_id: MEM-01
    description: Read-back of 0xDEADBEEF at base address 0x20000000 matches write
    expected: data == 0xDEADBEEF
  - id: CHK-MEM-02
    checklist_id: MEM-02
    description: All 32768 word addresses in 128 KB read back their written value
    expected: Zero mismatches in full address walk
  - id: CHK-MEM-03
    checklist_id: MEM-03
    description: All 32768 LFSR-pattern words compare correctly after burst write
    expected: Zero byte mismatches
  - id: CHK-MEM-04
    checklist_id: MEM-04
    description: DMA copy completes correctly and CPU data is uncorrupted
    expected: DMA destination matches source; CPU reads are consistent

pass_criteria:
  - All CHK-MEM checks PASS
  - No AHB HRESP=ERROR during SRAM access in valid range
  - DMA transfer-complete flag set within expected cycle count
